title: Australian Open announces record $111.5 million prize pool
publisher: Australian Open / Tennis Australia
notes: Official Australian Open article confirming the 2026 total prize pool of A$111.5m, headline singles payouts, and separate broader player-support and Summer of Tennis investment context.

title: Australian Open prize money increases more than 11 per cent in 2025
publisher: Australian Open / Tennis Australia
notes: Official Australian Open article confirming the 2025 total prize pool of A$96.5m, currency, and headline singles payouts.

title: Australian Open Prize Money 2021-2025
publisher: Tennis Australia
notes: Official PDF with 2021-2025 men's and women's singles per-player round payouts, per-event singles totals, total Australian Open prize-money lines including A$71.0m for 2021, and an all-figures-in-Australian-dollars note.

title: Australian Open prize money hits record high with $10 million increase in 2024
publisher: Australian Open / Tennis Australia
notes: Official Australian Open article confirming the 2024 total prize pool of A$86.5m, Australian-dollar currency, and headline singles, doubles, qualifying, and mixed-doubles payouts. The article discusses separate player facilities and services but does not identify a separate per-diem or player-support component within the A$86.5m prize pool.

title: 2020 Australian Open prize-money table
publisher: Wikipedia, citing Australian Open official prize-money release
notes: Secondary tournament page reporting Australian Open 2020 total prize money of A$71.0m and citing the official AO 'Record $71 million in prize money for Australian Open 2020' release. Used because the official archived release was not retrieved in this pass.

title: 2019 Australian Open prize-money table
publisher: Wikipedia, citing Australian Open official prize-money release
notes: Secondary tournament page reporting Australian Open 2019 total prize money of A$62.5m and citing the official AO 'Record $62.5 million in prize money for Australian Open 2019' release. Used because the official archived release was not retrieved in this pass.

title: 2018 Australian Open prize-money table
publisher: Wikipedia, citing Australian Open prize-money reporting
notes: Secondary tournament page reporting Australian Open 2018 total prize money of A$55.0m. Used as a medium-confidence historical numerator until an official archived AO/Tennis Australia page is retrieved.

title: 2017 Australian Open prize-money table
publisher: Wikipedia, citing Australian Open prize-money reporting
notes: Secondary tournament page reporting Australian Open 2017 total prize money of A$50.0m. Used as a medium-confidence historical numerator until an official archived AO/Tennis Australia page is retrieved.

title: 2025 French Open prize money table
publisher: Wikipedia, citing The Independent round-by-round prize-money report
notes: Retained for the historical men's-singles event row. The later tournament-total normalization uses the official 2025 Roland-Garros press kit instead.

title: French Open 2025 guide: How to watch on TV, betting odds and more to know about Roland-Garros
publisher: Associated Press
notes: AP reports total player compensation at the 2025 French Open of €56.352m and says that total includes per diems and money paid to former players taking part in exhibitions. Used as support-inclusive context, not as clean competition prize money.

title: Press Kit Roland Garros 2024
publisher: Federation Francaise de Tennis / Roland Garros
notes: Official Roland Garros 2024 press kit. It reports €53.478m total prize money and states that the total includes Legends Trophy prize money and Per Diem daily accommodation allowance, so the headline total is used only as support-inclusive context.

title: 2024 Roland Garros prize-money table
publisher: Wikipedia, citing the official Roland Garros 2024 press kit
notes: Secondary table citing the official press kit. It separates €51.260m for listed events above from €2.218m in other events plus estimated per diem, but because other events and per diem are bundled, it does not support a clean full tournament-total competition-prize row.

title: The Championships, Wimbledon 2026 Prize Money
publisher: The All England Lawn Tennis Club / Wimbledon
notes: Official one-page PDF with 2026 gentlemen's and ladies' singles per-player round payouts, per-event singles total, £62.55m total tennis events prize money, £1.65m estimated per diems, and £64.2m broader total prize money.

title: The Championships, Wimbledon 2025 Prize Money
publisher: The All England Lawn Tennis Club / Wimbledon
notes: Official one-page PDF with 2025 gentlemen's and ladies' singles per-player round payouts, per-event singles total, total tennis events prize money, estimated per diems, and broader total prize money.

notes: Official one-page PDF with 2024 gentlemen's and ladies' singles per-player round payouts, per-event singles total, total tennis events prize money, estimated per diems, and broader total prize money.

notes: Official one-page PDF with 2023 gentlemen's and ladies' singles per-player round payouts, per-event singles total, total tennis events prize money, estimated per diems, and broader total prize money.

notes: Official Companies House filing for AELTC Championships Ltd, the principal contracting party for The Championships. It reports 2023 turnover of £380.156m, operating profit of £53.776m, and explains the net available surplus division to LTA Operations.

notes: Official one-page PDF with 2022 gentlemen's and ladies' singles per-player round payouts, per-event singles total, total tennis events prize money, estimated per diems, and broader total prize money.

title: The All England Lawn Tennis Club (Championships) Limited full accounts made up to 31 July 2021
publisher: Companies House / The All England Lawn Tennis Club (Championships) Limited
notes: Official Companies House filing for AELTC Championships Ltd, the principal contracting party for The Championships. title: 2019 Wimbledon Championships prize-money table

title: The All England Lawn Tennis Club (Championships) Limited full accounts made up to 31 July 2019
publisher: Companies House / The All England Lawn Tennis Club (Championships) Limited
notes: Official Companies House filing for AELTC Championships Ltd, the principal contracting party for The Championships. It reports 2019 turnover of £292.071m, operating profit of £50.134m, and net available surplus from The Championships of £44.876m.

title: The All England Lawn Tennis Club (Championships) Limited full accounts made up to 31 July 2020
publisher: Companies House / The All England Lawn Tennis Club (Championships) Limited
notes: Official Companies House filing for AELTC Championships Ltd. The 2020 report gives comparative 2019 turnover of £292.071m, 2019 operating profit of £50.134m, and 2019 net available surplus of £44.876m. The 2020 year itself reflects cancellation of The Championships and includes insurance income, so the 2020 values are not normalized as a normal tournament denominator.

notes: Official Companies House filing for AELTC Championships Ltd, the principal contracting party for The Championships. It reports 2018 turnover of £254.873m, operating profit of £42.237m, and surplus from The Championships available for division of £37.785m.

publisher: Companies House / The All England Lawn Tennis Club (Championships) Limited
notes: Official Companies House filing for AELTC Championships Ltd, the principal contracting party for The Championships. It reports 2017 turnover of £216.145m, operating profit of £37.294m, and surplus from The Championships available for division of £33.637m.

title: 2025 US Open Prize Money
publisher: United States Tennis Association / US Open
notes: Official US Open prize-money page URL was reachable but did not expose parseable text to the research crawler; values are cross-checked with a secondary page citing this official source.

title: 2024 US Open prize money will be largest purse in tennis history
publisher: United States Tennis Association / US Open
notes: Official US Open release confirming $75m total player compensation for 2024 and listing the main draw singles, main draw doubles, qualifying singles, and mixed doubles payout breakdown. Direct HTTP/crawler fetches were not parseable, but the rendered official page text was verified in-browser. The release also identifies a Wheelchair Championships player grant because that event was not staged in 2024 due to the Paralympic Games.

title: 2024 US Open prize-money table
publisher: Wikipedia, citing the official US Open prize-money release
notes: Secondary cross-check that the listed 2024 US Open event payout rows sum to $68.756m and that the remaining $6.244m of the $75.0m total is per diem. Used only as corroboration for keeping the clean competition-prize subtotal separate from total player compensation.

title: 2022 US Open Prize Money
publisher: United States Tennis Association / US Open
notes: Archived official US Open prize-money page for 2022. The archive URL and official page title were verified in-browser, but the archived body did not expose usable text in this environment, so the clean split is cross-checked through a secondary table citing this official page.

title: 2022 US Open prize-money table
publisher: Wikipedia, citing the official US Open prize-money page and ATP Tour
notes: Secondary split between $56.4981m in listed professional event payouts, $1.032m in wheelchair event payouts, $2.5719m in per diem, and $60.102m total prize money. Used as corroboration for keeping per diem separate from the clean competition-prize subtotal.

title: United States Tennis Association Incorporated and Affiliates consolidated financial statements, years ended December 31, 2022 and 2021
publisher: United States Tennis Association / BDO USA LLP
notes: Audited consolidated financial statements. The consolidated statement of activities reports US Open operating revenue of $472.172m for 2022 and $406.172m for 2021, with amounts stated in thousands of dollars, and separately reports US Open direct expenses plus depreciation, pledge, and debt-interest expense. No explicit US Open profit/surplus line is normalized in this slice.

title: 2021 US Open offers record prize money, $57.5 million in total player compensation
publisher: United States Tennis Association / US Open
notes: Official US Open release confirming a rounded $57.5m in 2021 prize money and total player compensation, nearly $6m for qualifying, and prize-money increases across singles, doubles, mixed doubles, and wheelchair competitions. Rendered official page text was verified in-browser.

title: 2021 US Open prize-money table
publisher: Wikipedia, citing the official US Open prize-money release
notes: Secondary split between $53.75944m in listed singles, doubles, mixed doubles, and qualifying event payouts, $0.600m in other competition events, $3.10256m in estimated per diem, and $57.462m total. Used as corroboration for keeping per diem separate from the clean competition-prize subtotal.

title: 2025 US Open prize money sets record for largest purse in tennis history
publisher: United States Tennis Association / US Open
notes: Official US Open release confirming $90m total player compensation for 2025 and a separate $5m in travel and hotel support before the prize-money breakdown. Direct HTTP/crawler fetches were not parseable, but the rendered official page text was verified in-browser.

title: US Open singles champions will get a record $5 million in 2025 and total compensation is up 20%
publisher: Associated Press
notes: Parseable corroboration that USTA announced $85 million in 2025 prize money across singles, doubles, qualifying, and wheelchair events, plus $90 million in total player compensation.

title: US Open 2025 prize money table
publisher: Wikipedia, citing the official US Open prize-money page
notes: Secondary cross-check used because the official US Open page did not provide crawler-readable text in this environment.

title: United States Tennis Association Incorporated and Affiliates consolidated financial statements, years ended December 31, 2018 and 2017
publisher: United States Tennis Association / BDO USA LLP
notes: Audited consolidated financial statements. PDF page 7 reports US Open operating revenue of $380.144m for 2018 and $347.593m for 2017, with amounts stated in thousands of dollars. No explicit US Open tournament profit or surplus is reported.

title: United States Tennis Association Incorporated and Affiliates consolidated financial statements, years ended December 31, 2020 and 2019
publisher: United States Tennis Association / BDO USA LLP
notes: Audited consolidated financial statements. PDF page 7 reports US Open operating revenue of $181.210m for 2020 and $399.601m for 2019, with amounts stated in thousands of dollars. No explicit US Open tournament profit or surplus is reported.

title: United States Tennis Association Incorporated and Affiliates consolidated financial statements, years ended December 31, 2024 and 2023
publisher: United States Tennis Association / BDO USA, P.C.
notes: Audited consolidated financial statements. PDF page 6 reports US Open operating revenue of $559.658m for 2024 and $514.105m for 2023, with amounts stated in thousands of dollars. The audit reports tournament expense lines but no explicit US Open tournament profit or surplus.

title: 2017 US Open Prize Money to Top $50 Million
publisher: United States Tennis Association / US Open
notes: Official release confirming the 2017 player-compensation headline and round payouts. Used with the archived official-table transcription and separate wheelchair total to exclude per diem and the Champions Invitational.

title: 2017 US Open prize-money table
publisher: Wikipedia, citing the archived official US Open prize-money page
notes: Transcribes $48.3868m in listed professional-event payouts, $0.600m in other events, $1.478m estimated per diem, and $50.4648m total. A separate event source reports $0.200m wheelchair prize money, allowing the $0.400m Champions Invitational component to be excluded from the strict competition numerator.

title: 2018 US Open Prize Money to Reach $53 Million
publisher: United States Tennis Association / US Open
notes: Official release with round-by-round singles and doubles payouts and the $53m player-compensation headline.

title: 2018 US Open prize-money table
publisher: Wikipedia, citing the archived official US Open prize-money page
notes: Reports $50.56584m in listed professional-draw payouts and $0.350m in wheelchair prize money. The clean $50.91584m subtotal excludes the remaining support, per-diem, or non-competition portion of the $53m headline.

title: 2019 US Open Prize Money - Fact Sheet
publisher: United States Tennis Association / US Open
notes: Official one-page fact sheet reports $53.5175m in listed professional draws, $0.650m bundled across wheelchair competition and Legends, $1m player pension, $2.0712m estimated per diem, and $57.2387m total player compensation. Wheelchair and Legends are inseparable, so a strict full competition numerator remains unavailable.

title: 2020 US Open Player Handbook
publisher: United States Tennis Association / US Open
notes: Printed page 53 provides the pandemic-format payout schedule. Draw-weighted singles and doubles payouts plus the stated $0.350m wheelchair allocation total $43.662m, excluding player relief, international relief, and direct hotel payments.

title: 2023 US Open prize money and player compensation to total $65 million
publisher: United States Tennis Association / US Open
notes: Official release provides main-draw payouts and identifies per diem, travel vouchers, hotel, meals, and racquet stringing as player-expense assistance included in the broader package.

title: 2023 US Open prize-money table
publisher: Wikipedia, citing the archived official US Open prize-money page and ATP
notes: Reports $58.9768m in listed professional events, $1.3668m in wheelchair events, and $4.65642m in per diem/support. The clean competition subtotal is $60.3436m.

title: Dossier de presse Roland-Garros 2025
publisher: Federation Francaise de Tennis / Roland-Garros
notes: Pages 33-34 provide complete competition payout tables and state that the EUR56.352m headline includes Legends and per diem. The listed competition tables sum to EUR54.03217m.

title: Roland-Garros 2023: le prize money devoile
publisher: Federation Francaise de Tennis / Roland-Garros
notes: Official article and embedded category tables for singles, qualifying, doubles, mixed, wheelchair, and quad competition payouts. The tables sum to EUR47.515m, excluding the EUR2.085m headline residual.

title: Roland Garros Announces 2022 Prize Money
publisher: ATP Tour, reporting Roland-Garros prize schedules
notes: Provides official main singles and doubles schedules. Mixed, wheelchair, and quad categories were cross-checked against the complete FFT-communicated Sport Buzz Business table; all competition categories sum to EUR43.5806m.

title: Roland-Garros 2022 - le detail du prize money record devoile
publisher: Sport Buzz Business, reporting FFT-communicated schedules
notes: Complete category table used for qualifying, mixed, wheelchair, and quad payouts omitted from the ATP summary.

title: Roland Garros Reveals 2020 Prize Money Distribution
publisher: ATP Tour, reporting Roland-Garros prize schedules
notes: Provides official qualifying, main-draw singles, and doubles schedules. Para categories were cross-checked against the complete tournament table; competition categories sum to EUR36.409676m after excluding EUR2m in support.

title: Le detail du prize money de Roland-Garros 2020
publisher: Sport Buzz Business, reporting tournament schedules
notes: Complete category table used to add wheelchair and quad payouts and to keep the explicit EUR2m support component outside competition prize money.

title: Roland-Garros 2019: la dotation globale connue
publisher: Federation Francaise de Tennis / Roland-Garros
notes: Official article and category images provide complete singles, qualifying, doubles, mixed, wheelchair, and quad schedules. The competition tables sum to EUR40.671m, excluding the EUR1.990m Legends/per-diem residual.

title: Focus Tennis & Marketing - Roland-Garros 2024
publisher: SPORSORA, citing Federation Francaise de Tennis
notes: FFT-attributed tournament turnover series used for 2020 EUR147.0m, 2021 EUR184.5m, 2022 EUR308.0m, and 2023 EUR328.0m. The 2022 value conflicts with a separate EUR325m report, so its denominator remains visibly caveated.

title: Proces verbal - Assemblee generale 14 decembre 2024
publisher: Federation Francaise de Tennis
notes: Official FFT financial report states Roland-Garros products/revenue of EUR345.7m and tournament charges of EUR201.5m. It does not label the EUR144.2m difference as tournament profit or surplus, so profit remains unavailable.

title: Leading tennis players including Djokovic and Sabalenka unhappy with French Open prize money
publisher: The Guardian, reporting a player statement
notes: Reports Roland-Garros 2025 tournament revenue of EUR395m from a player statement. Used as a secondary revenue denominator with attribution caveat; no tournament profit is supplied.

title: Roland Garros 2017 - Le detail de toutes les primes du tournoi
publisher: Sportune / 20 Minutes
notes: Contemporaneous main singles, qualifying, and doubles schedules. Mixed and wheelchair rows require a complete tournament-table cross-check, so the derived EUR34.067m clean total remains medium confidence and provisional.

title: Roland-Garros 2018 - Le prize money devoile
publisher: Sport Buzz Business, reporting Roland-Garros schedules
notes: Complete professional schedules through mixed doubles. Wheelchair amounts are reconstructed from later official change disclosures and a secondary event table, so the EUR37.3452m clean total remains medium confidence and provisional.

title: Proces-verbal de l'Assemblee Generale, 19-20 janvier 2019 - Exercice 2018
publisher: Federation Francaise de Tennis
notes: Official FFT analytical P&L for the Internationaux de France. Digital PDF page 63 of 79, printed page 122, reports 2017 products EUR222.430m, charges EUR136.950m, solde analytique EUR85.480m; and 2018 products EUR225.010m, charges EUR144.123m, solde analytique EUR80.887m. La Griffe Roland-Garros is separately reported.

title: Proces-verbal de l'Assemblee Generale, 14-15 decembre 2019
publisher: Federation Francaise de Tennis, mirrored by Ligue de Tennis de Nouvelle-Caledonie
notes: FFT general-meeting material on an affiliated regional-league mirror. Digital PDF page 86 of 91, printed pages 168-169, reports 2019 products EUR247.265m, charges EUR156.675m, and solde analytique EUR90.590m in the Internationaux de France analytical P&L. La Griffe is separately reported.

title: 2017 US Open prize-money and wheelchair-event summary
publisher: Wikipedia, citing USTA/US Open sources
notes: Reports total 2017 US Open wheelchair-event prize money of $0.200m. Used to separate wheelchair competition from the archived official page's $0.600m Champions Invitational/other-events bundle.

title: 2017 French Open complete prize-money table
